notes: Eighth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school will foster community, generate enthusiasm, challenge students academically, and produce successful citizens.
Its vision calls for a community of empowered learners in an atmosphere of mutual respect, responsibility, and trust.
The school says it opened as a charter school in 2001 and began with about 275 students before adding one grade level each year.
Official FAQ materials say the school's board includes both community members and parent-elected members so parents have a voice in the future of education there.
The school says students have taken field trips across the United States and overseas to places such as Spain, Italy, France, Ireland, and the Bahamas.
Official directory pages list Heather Vereyken as Executive Director and Chad Huffines as Board Chairman.

Its mission says the school prepares students to be self-motivated, lifelong, independent, and collaborative learners through a challenging STEAM-focused curriculum in an innovative and supportive environment.
Its published vision says the school nurtures how to think, not what to think, one child at a time.
Official materials say students learn through an integrated, place-based curriculum and become stewards of their environment and community.
The school's website says it earned a 10 Year Charter Renewal and also received grants from Charter School Growth Fund, NC GreenPower Solar plus Schools, and the EPA for electric buses.
A board page says the founder and managing director is Toni Shellady.
The newer board page lists Lisa Gonzalez as board president.
Its mission says the school provides innovative instruction through experiential learning with a focus on STEAM.
Its vision says students will be prepared to pursue STEAM-related careers and become lifelong learners through project-based learning.
The school says it was founded in 2014 by A. Monica Cutno, Marla Mills-Wilson, and Portia Scott.
Its homepage notes that it was recognized by the State Board of Education as a North Carolina Model STEM School of Distinction.
Official pages say the school is committed to inclusive and equitable learning and calls this approach "The ESA Way."
The administration page lists Dr. Charles Fuller as Executive Director and the board page lists Monique Taylor as board president.
Its public-facing theme line is "Nurturing Potential – Inspiring Excellence – Developing Leaders."
The school says it is focused on preparing students to meet the requirements and demands of college.
Its staff page says instructors bring new and innovative approaches to learning and emphasizes that every student matters.
The governing board page says the board is the policy-making body with overall responsibility for curriculum, budget, facilities, and staff employment.
Official governance information says board meetings are held on the second Thursday of each month at 7:00 p.m.
Official pages list Andrew Swanner as Executive Director, with MaryAnn Crews and Ashley Barnette serving as Lower School and Upper School Directors.
Its mission says the school offers a superior college preparatory educational option for the community and its families.
Its core values are organized around three words: Academic, Character, and Community.
The school says it provides a challenging college preparatory curriculum in an environment of high expectations complemented by artistic and extracurricular activities.
Its portrait of a graduate says students are expected to be thoughtful communicators, intellectually curious, and exposed to the arts.
The board page lists Melia Mauldin as president of the board.
The leaders page says Jennifer Killen serves as Head of School and describes her leadership style as servant leadership.
